messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Support Create PR Operations in Chagesets and Changes View of Agent Window
ISSUE BODY: 

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### 7.6 PR readiness  
- [ ] PR title: `agentHost/claude: Phase 4 — ClaudeAgent skeleton`.
- [ ] PR description links to [roadmap.md](./roadmap.md) Phase 4 section and notes the exit criteria are met.
- [ ] PR description lists the stubbed methods + their target phase as a table.
- [ ] PR is opened as draft until the build passes; promote when green.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Role and Objective  
You are a developer working on the 'agents window'. Your goal is to make changes to the agents window (`src/vs/sessions`), minimally editing outside of that directory.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Managing Subagents  
Use the [`/agents`](#agents--create-and-manage-subagents) slash command to create, view, edit, and delete subagents.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Overview  
The view displays a hierarchical tree structure:  
```
AI Customization (View Container)
└── AI Customization (Tree View)
├── Custom Agents (.agent.md files)
│ ├── Workspace
│ │ └── agent files...
│ ├── User
│ │ └── agent files...
│ └── Extensions
│ └── agent files...
├── Skills (SKILL.md files)
│ └── (same storage structure)
├── Instructions (.instructions.md files)
│ └── (same storage structure)
└── Prompts (.prompt.md files)
└── (same storage structure)
```  
**Key Features:**
- 3-level tree hierarchy: Category → Storage Group → Files
- Auto-expands category nodes on initial load and refresh to show storage groups
- Symbol-based root element for type safety
- Double-click to open files in editor
- Context menu support with Open and Run Prompt actions
- Toolbar actions: New dropdown, Refresh, Collapse All
- Skill names parsed from frontmatter with fallback to folder name
- Responsive to IPromptsService change events

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### 7.7 PR readiness  
- [ ] PR title: `agentHost/claude: Phase 5 — session lifecycle`.
- [ ] PR description links to [roadmap.md](roadmap.md) Phase 5 and to this plan; notes that exit criteria are met.
- [ ] PR description lists the 7 implemented stubs + the 9 still-stubbed methods + their target phase as a table.
- [ ] PR description calls out the Phase-6 contract notes (worktree-extraction prerequisite, `canUseTool` consumes `permissionMode` + `Permissions` directly — see §8).
- [ ] PR is opened as draft until the build passes; promote when green.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Support Create PR Operations in Changesets and Changes View of Agent Window",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-window", "pr-operations", "changesets", "feature"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As an AI agent developer, I want to create Pull Request operations from both the Changesets view and Changes View in the Agent Window so that I can review and commit changes as PRs for my custom agents and configurations.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a user is viewing the Changesets view in the Agent Window, When they click 'Create PR Operation', Then a modal/form appears allowing them to specify PR title, description, and target branch",
    "Given a user is viewing the Changes View for an agent file, When they select 'Create PR Operation' from context menu or toolbar, Then a PR operation form opens with pre-filled file information",
    "The Create PR Operation modal must validate required fields (title, description)",
    "PR operations created via Changesets view should aggregate multiple changes if applicable",
    "PR operations created via Changes View should be scoped to the selected file(s)",
    "Created PR operations must appear in a dedicated 'My PR Operations' list with status tracking"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Create PROperation service layer for creating and retrieving PR operations",
      "Implement API endpoint POST /api/agent/pr-operations to create new PR operation",
      "Add validation logic for PR operation data (title length, description format)",
      "Update PR operation entity model with status tracking fields"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Create 'Create PR Operation' modal component in Agent Window",
      "Implement Changesets view PR operation button/action",
      "Implement Changes View context menu action for PR operations",
      "Add 'My PR Operations' list panel to display created operations with status"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test Create PR Operation modal opens correctly from both views",
      "Validate form submission handles empty/invalid inputs gracefully",
      "Verify PR operations appear in 'My PR Operations' list after creation",
      "Test concurrent PR operation creation scenarios",
      "Verify proper error handling for network failures"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "All backend API endpoints implemented and tested",
    "Frontend components fully functional with proper styling",
    "Unit tests covering all new code paths (80%+ coverage)",
    "Integration tests for PR operation creation flow",
    "Code reviewed by senior engineer",
    "Documentation updated for PR operations feature"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected data structure for a PR operation (title, description, files changed, branch target)?",
    "Should PR operations support multiple file selections from Changes View?",
    "Is there an existing PR operation management system we should integrate with or build from scratch?",
    "What status values should PR operations track (pending, reviewing, merged, rejected)?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "Users have basic VS Code knowledge and understand what a Pull Request is",
    "The Agent Window already has proper file selection capabilities in Changes View",
    "There will be an existing mechanism to actually create PRs from these operations (separate feature)",
    "PR operations are meant for internal agent development workflows"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend API Implementation",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Create service layer and REST endpoints for PR operations CRUD"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend Modal Component",
      "priority": "medium",
      "description": "Build the Create PR Operation modal with form validation"
    },
    {
      "name": "UI Integration",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Add buttons/actions in Changesets and Changes views to trigger PR operation creation"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {
      "task_group": "Backend API Implementation",
      "estimated_days": 2,
      "assignee_role": "backend developer"
    },
    {
      "task_group": "Frontend Modal Component",
      "estimated_days": 1.5,
      "assignee_role": "frontend developer"
    },
    {
      "task_group": "UI Integration",
      "estimated_days": 2,
      "assignee_role": "frontend developer"
    }
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - PR readiness section (Phase 4)",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Managing Subagents section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Overview section (tree structure)"
  ]
}
```
